title: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — full lesson set
target_form: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — choosing between them based on time reference and present relevance
methodology_notes: Four-tier sequence (awareness → controlled → meaningful). Single context (travel) for episodic anchoring. Form description follows Murphy EGiU 5e Units 7–14; contrast rule from Swan PEU §455–456.
sources: Murphy, English Grammar in Use 5e — Units 7–14
Ur, Grammar Practice Activities — Present Perfect chapter
English Grammar Profile — B1 Present Perfect descriptors

exercises.rubric: Read the email. Underline Present Perfect verb forms in BLUE and Past Simple verb forms in RED. Then discuss: which tense does the writer use to describe the trip *as a whole*, and which to describe specific moments?
exercises.context: Anna has just got back from a trip to Spain.

items.text: Hi Sam! I've just got home from Spain — what a trip! We've been to Madrid, Seville and Granada. The food has been incredible. In Seville, we tried real paella for the first time, and in Granada we visited the Alhambra. I haven't unpacked yet, but I'll send photos soon.
items.follow_up: Past Simple is used for specific finished moments (where, when). Present Perfect frames the trip as a current experience whose effects persist (still excited, still unpacked).

exercises.rubric: Complete each sentence with the verb in brackets in the Present Perfect or Past Simple.

items.explanation: Present Perfect for life-experience count (no specific time).
Past Simple — 'last week' is finished time.
Present Perfect for life-experience question with 'ever'.

exercises.rubric: Choose the option that best completes each sentence.

items.stem: I ____ to Spain twice — once in 2018 and again last year.

distractor_diagnostics.B: Past Simple, but the sentence sums up life experience (twice).

items.explanation: Present Perfect 'have been' for cumulative life experience.

exercises.rubric: Complete the prompts truthfully about yourself. Use the tense that matches the time reference.

items.stem: A country I have visited: ____. The last time I went there: ____.
items.criterion: Present Perfect for the experience; Past Simple with finished time for the visit.
items.sample_answer: A country I have visited: Italy. The last time I went there: in 2022.
